comment.body: CPI seems like a big number, but folks maybe dont know but US manufacturing been in recession for a while. Biden massive deficit spending  was kept US out of a recession, Government and government adjacent jobs provided 80% of the employment. Many CEO's saying we are in recession. DOGE reckons it has identified $300B in cuts, some of which will have been implemented...thats 1% of GDP ...in 2 months...so an annualised rate of 6%... US probably cant avoid recession. Powell faced with economy in decline (and maybe some one off price increases)  hold up rates given that backdrop (i dont think so) and  Truflation is 1.4%...there is little inflation. CPI will be interesting

comment.body:
Seems to me that a majority of people believe this is short term, as Alma probably does. However, it seems the true capitulation, as Alma also alluded to, will occur when everyone is convinced that this is a longer term problem that will send the US economy into a deflationary tail spin.

What data will be needed to confirm that? And how long until there is a visible trend in the data?

So 1.5 months seems to be a tall order to convince everyone else of the latter. We will only be through one quarter of earnings and have 1 month of employment data.

But maybe 1.5 months of this uncertainty and stress will be enough to bring everyone to their knees, along with the EU throwing a curve ball with their retaliations. Crazy environment.

comment.body:
Alma this is a key post.  I'm still trying to figure things out.  The answer is in here.  But maybe giving us hints would help.  I read through every single one of your posts and every daily chat until today.  I'm still not up to speed and am not a non-technical person so I should be able to understand.  

The answer is in here. but I have questions that I would love feedback on from the rest of the chat or yourself.   

The aggregated speed profile is positive with a net slop of 16.63%. 
The net centroid is 4859.11 (looks familiar?)
If long speed dynamics and shorter spot/vol beta are getting realized here, the downside will be hit.
The downside pivot is 4570.95
and the upside pivot is 4900.72; and the momentum has a chance to stabilize only above 5121.58 if spot/vol beta realizes down.
We have a zero vanna line at 4924.42 with positive vanna convexity, so low intraday vol is needed to break above this line.
Charm flip is at 4992.48 with negative convexity (color). This environment mechanically doesn’t favor for vol decline.
Options market is a bit shorter skew.

comment.body: I think people are making too much of the recent price action in USTs. Yes it has been sudden and violent but its adjusting to the possible realignment of global flows and doing it relatively smoothly. I expect 10y auction to go well today. Some nice term and risk premium built in which should entice buyers.
